Q7: Welcome in this study on social behavior     This study is part of a research project about social behavior. It is being conducted by Coralie Chevallier at Ecole Normale Superieure (Paris).     During this study you will play games with different virtual partners. These “virtual partners” are not real people but are programs that can simulate real-life behavior, just like characters in videogames. In each game you will be matched with another virtual partner who behaves independently, so you will never play with the same virtual partner twice. You will have to make decisions that affect the payoff of yourself and the virtual partner.       The study will take place over a 3-week period. On the first day (today), you will have to take part in an initial study that will take around 10 minutes. Then you will be contacted every other day during 3 weeks to take part in 8 intermediary studies. Each intermediate study will last 5 minutes. On the last day of the study, you will take part in a final study that will take around 10 minutes.     Here is a detailed calender that you can download if needed.      Download calender     Participation in this study is voluntary, and you may withdraw at any time, but you will only be compensated if you complete the entire study (i.e., all 10 days). After completing the entire study, you will receive a fixed compensation of 7.5 pounds and you will receive an additional bonus payment based on your performance of one of the games per day (these games will be chosen at random).    Your data will be fully anonymized and stored on an open science platform. Our data storage procedure follows all General Data Protection Regulations. There are no known risks for participating in this study.       To get further instructions about the study, please check the box 'Read, understood and approved' below, and then click on the next button. By checking this box, you indicate that you have read, understood and approved this statement and are over the age of 18.
